expected_signals.summary: Frames external-database analytics as constrained by a driver-layer bottleneck unsuited to bulk columnar reads.
Introduces Jailbreak as a database-bypass approach that reads storage files directly and materializes columnar buffers without going through the database engine.
Uses LLM-assisted code synthesis to regenerate database storage readers from source code and documentation rather than hand-built parsing logic.
Evaluates the approach on PostgreSQL and MySQL storage files and validates correctness against JDBC/ODBC-based baselines on TPC-H query results.
Reports major end-to-end analytical throughput gains, with speedups up to 27x.
Claims the methodology can break database lock-in beyond the two evaluated systems when file formats are available from documentation or source code.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: breaking data lock-in across database systems

notes: Observable proof for this labeling task is limited to source-text grounding: each anchor_quote is a verbatim substring from the provided abstract. 'Jailbreak' is the method name here, not a security incident; the paper explicitly centers database bypass and lock-in.
